issue_title: Pass extension/flags as list of strings instead of using EXT_* and HTML_* constants
issue_body:
I cought myself making a mapping (dict) to the `EXT_*` and `HTML_*` constants whenever I want to let the user control the extensions and flags. And also saw the same pattern in an other user's code.

I think it's easier to pass a list or tuple to the renderer and parser with the extensions and flags as strings in there. For example:

~~~ python
print m.html('A ~~complex~~ simple example.',
    extensions=('striketrough', 'superscript'))
~~~

Instead of:

~~~ python
print m.html('A ~~complex~~ simple example.',
    extensions=m.EXT_STRIKETHROUGH | m.EXT_SUPERSCRIPT)
~~~

The downside of this is an API change.

Opinions?

issue_title: Non-Crockford's Base32 letters converted differently in Java or Python implementations
issue_body:
Hi Andrew, 

first of all, thanks for the amazing library, we've been using a lot!

I have a doubt regarding how we fix the conversion of ULIDs which are not following Crockford's Base32 standard.

We are using Lua to generate some guids (https://github.com/Tieske/ulid.lua) and for some reason, we get from time to time letters outside the Crockford's Base32. 
While trying to fix this on our side (we're not sure how this is happening to be honest), we realised that Java and Python implementations silently corrects this issue in different ways:

### Java
```java
ULID.Value ulidValueFromString = ULID.parseULID("01BX73KC0TNH409RTFD1JXKmO0")
--> "01BX73KC0TNH409RTFD1JXKM00"
```
`mO` is silently converted into `M0`

### Python
```python
In [1]: import ulid

In [2]: u = ulid.from_str('01BX73KC0TNH409RTFD1JXKmO0')

In [3]: u
Out[3]: <ULID('01BX73KC0TNH409RTFD1JXKQZ0')>

In [4]: u.str
Out[4]: '01BX73KC0TNH409RTFD1JXKQZ0'
```
`mO` is silently converted into `QZ`

Shouldn't the python library behave as the Java one as per the [Crockford's Base32](http://crockford.com/wrmg/base32.html) spec, converting `L` and `I` to `1` and `O` to `0` and only upper casing lower case letters instead of changing them?

Thanks a lot in advance!

Eddie

issue_title: Payload protocol checksum calculation broken for IPv6 parsing
issue_body:
_From [aa...@monkey.org](https://code.google.com/u/116105672249653840815/) on January 07, 2011 09:41:28_

There are a couple problems.  One is that you don't want to look at ip6.nxt to check the layer 4 protocol, because if there are extension headers, you need to walk the chain to find it.  That is already done in unpack(), where ip6.p is set to the payload protocol, so we can use that.

Perhaps this is a separate issue, but there is also a bug in the icmp6 unpack, which causes checksum calculation failure for icmp6 in many cases.  The problem is dpkt doesn't have handlers for all possible icmp6 types (for example, missing important ones like neighbour solitication == 135).  In this case the key lookup fails in _typesw, but you don't want to set self.data, because this will throw off the length calculation and you will end up with bad icmp6 checksums.

Note: The second problem was fixed for v4 over 3 years ago in icmp.py by jono (via r45 ).

Diff attached w/ test.

**Attachment:** [dpkt-ipv6-cksum-fill.diff](http://code.google.com/p/dpkt/issues/detail?id=59)

_Original issue: http://code.google.com/p/dpkt/issues/detail?id=59_

issue_title: Disable warnings when overwriting output file
issue_body:
There are scenarios where you know in advance that you're going to use pysox to overwrite an existing file, e.g. when using temporary files like in [this example](https://github.com/rabitt/pysox/issues/6#issuecomment-251792138) from #6. In this situation it's inconvenient to have pysox issue a warning for every file overwrite (as there are many), and right now there's no way to disable these warnings in pysox (you can hack it by changing the logging level as in the example provided above, but that's not ideal).

It would be nice to have an optional flag in pysox (or maybe at the transformer/combiner level?) to disable file overwrite warnings (and maybe another option to disable all warnings from pysox?).

issue_title: Windows file manager and OSX Finder fails on file names with comma
issue_body:
One of our users wanted to upload a bunch of files with several exotic characters in file names. We managed to change our own code base to support most except ones with commas. 
Apparently the native Microsoft Win 7 WebDAV client does not URL-encode paths passed in the 
HTTP_AUTHORIZATION header and that causes troubles with the headerparser regex in http_authenticator.py.

An example HTTP_AUTHORIZATION value would be

```
'Digest
username="bardino@...",realm="/",nonce="MTQ...",uri="/test-enc/a,b.txt",cnonce="647...",nc=00000001,algorithm=MD5,response="af...",qop="auth"'
```

The regexp headerparser thus splits after the comma in /test-enc/a,b.txt causing auth errors like:
```
<140650507945728> [10:53:59.701] wsgidav.http_authenticator:
computeDigestResponse('/', 'bardino@...', ...):
4177dc6ee07fcaa2455855132cdeb7f8 != af0259a96cfd680c478b875e4b44e4f1
```

Testing the same with the cadaver client works and debug show that the paths get urlencoded there.

I'll see if I can provide a server side hotfix to address it.

issue_title: REUSABLE connection strategy creates too many connections
issue_body:
With 2.5.0, if I create a connection with REUSABLE client_strategy eg:

    server = Server(args.server)
    connection = Connection(ServerPool([server]), args.bind_dn, args.bind_pw, client_strategy=REUSABLE)

every query would create 2 new connections, one for checking availability and one for fetching schema (this latter connection is even created when the server is constructed with `get_info="NO_INFO"`, then it only binds the connection and closes it). Thus a simple piece of code with 3 queries creates a total of 8 TCP connections, eg:

   mid = connection.search(...)
   connection.get_response(mid)
   mid = connection.search(...)
   connection.get_response(mid)
   mid = connection.search(...)
   connection.get_response(mid)

results in the following network operations:

1. *Connection 1*, closed immediately
1. *Connection 2*, bind, fetching schema, closed
1. *Connection 3*, closed immediately
1. *Connection 4*, bind, fetching schema, doing first search, not closed
1. *Connection 5*, closed immediately
1. *Connection 6*, bind, doing second search, not closed
1. *Connection 7*, closed immediately
1. *Connection 8*, bind, doing third search, not closed

If the connection strategy is changed to ASYNC, the exact same code creates only 2 connections: the availability check and the actual LDAP connection.

I can provide you with the actual test code, but it is really not much more than the snippets here plus boilterplate.

issue_title: Cache parts of files instead of full files
issue_body:
I am tying to adopt yas3fs for processing of large files chunk-by-chunk in parallel on several nodes.
Each instance reads only a part of the file, producing result intended for further aggregation elsewhere. Kind of map-reduce. The software I use was developed by other guys (namely NCBI), and unfortunately can read data only from files on disk. It is impossible to rewrite it to access data directly from S3.

The problem is that yas3fs allocates full size records for files in memory or on disk (depending on the cache size).

Even if I set disk cache sizes to zero yas3fs creates files with the size of a full file.

It would be great if yas3fs could only cache parts of the files that were used instead of creating full records for them starting from certain threshold file size.

issue_title: regression target of shape predictor trainer
issue_body: I am confused about the regression target when training a shape predictor model. In your code, the target is the difference of ground truth and current shape. According to this paper, [Face Alignment by Explicit Shape Regression](http://research.microsoft.com/pubs/206636/Face%20Alignment%20by%20Explicit%20Shape%20Regression.pdf), however, the real target is product of difference and similarity transformation matrix, which has been computed when extracting pixel values. Have you ever considered that? It may influence the performance.

issue_title: Add check to collapse "redundant" segments in surface-surface intersection
issue_body:
For example, in 0106ac81e39080cb868c9827298f83374c75f246 (before a fix that followed in the next commit).

```python
>>> surface1.nodes
array([[ 0. ,  0.5,  1. ,  0.2,  0.7,  0. ],
       [ 0. , -0.2,  0. ,  0.5,  0.7,  1. ]])
>>> surface2.nodes
array([[ 0.5   ,  2.0625,  3.625 , -1.0625,  0.5   , -2.625 ],
       [-0.125 ,  0.1875,  0.5   ,  0.1875,  0.5   ,  0.5   ]])
>>> intersection, = surface1.intersect(surface2)
>>> intersection
<CurvedPolygon (num_sides=5)>
>>> pprint.pprint(intersection._metadata)
((2, 0.5, 1.0),
 (0, 0.0, 0.75),
 (0, 0.75, 1.0),
 (1, 0.0, 0.4037087982163741),
 (4, 0.46918681542923973, 0.5640000000000001))
```

I.e. the two segments `(0, 0.0, 0.75)` and `(0, 0.75, 1.0)` can be "collapsed" into `(0, 0.0, 1.0)`. (See [intersection image][1].)

[1]: https://github.com/dhermes/bezier/blob/7538e3a622a91317451f241bf1f99b1fd2b8e878/docs/images/surfaces46Q_and_47Q.png

issue_title: Add exception type for errors in operators
prettyPrint does not work for Bits
issue_body:
I'm trying to prettyPrint a Bits object, and I get:
```
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pyasn1/type/base.py", line 380, in prettyPrint
    return self.prettyOut(self._value)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pysnmp/proto/rfc1902.py", line 660, in prettyOut
    name = self.__namedValues.getName(i * 8 + 7 - j)
AttributeError: 'Bits' object has no attribute '_Bits__namedValues'
```

issue_title: Type of license
issue_body:
Hi Philippe,

I think it would be good if you include the type of license in you repo. Especially it is important since this library in most of the cases imply commercial and proprietary use, and you definitely don't want to have any liabilities to those who use your code ;-) By default with no-license your code is protected by the copyright, so: "This means that you retain all rights to your source code and that nobody else may reproduce, distribute, or create derivative works from your work." 

Unfortunately I could not advise you much here - I'm not a legal specialist. But you can have a look at a rather good summary here: https://tldrlegal.com/ or here: http://choosealicense.com/. Also there's a FAQ here: https://help.github.com/articles/open-source-licensing/.

Personally, I prefer non-restrictive open-source licenses, and license my code under [MIT license](https://tldrlegal.com/license/mit-license). One of the drawback of restrictive GPL-type of licenses is a requirement to keep the license type and pass source code together with binaries when distributing, which is often might be a "no-go" for financial applications.

Best regards
Vladimir

issue_title: Support for iCal recurrence rules (RRULE)
issue_body:
I would like to add support for schedules defined with [RRules](http://dateutil.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/dateutil/rrule.html#rrule) based on the iCal RFC (https://icalendar.org/iCalendar-RFC-5545/3-8-5-3-recurrence-rule.html). I think the scope of work involved would be:
1. Implement a `RRuleSchedule` class that inherits from `celery.schedules.BaseSchedule` and implements `remaining_estimate` and `is_due` methods (using `dateutil.rrule`)
1. Update `RedBeatJSONDecoder` and `RedBeatJSONEncoder` to handle the new class
1. To handle RRules that end after a certain date/after a certain number of occurrences, add a way to mark schedules as 'inactive' after its last occurrence, so Redbeat will ignore them. Not sure exactly what this looks like yet

@sibson How does this sound to you? Does the scope of work look accurate or is there anything else that would be needed? Interested in your thoughts on how to best implement point 3.

I'm actively working on a project using Redbeat, and having RRULE support directly in Redbeat would make things easier for my use case (avoid having to convert RRULEs to cron timings, being able to let Redbeat take care of schedules that end after some time instead of adding logic externally). If you think this is worth doing in mainline Redbeat I can have a WIP PR later this week for you to look at.

issue_title: Feature: Image Selection / Filtering
issue_body:
Selecting only a certain type/volume/etc of manga chapters has been asked about a couple of times (https://github.com/mikf/gallery-dl/issues/25, https://github.com/mikf/gallery-dl/issues/11#issuecomment-309727059) and using metadata, which is usually only used to build filenames, to select/filter images or image-groups seems like a generally useful feature.

The foundation for this has already been put into place with https://github.com/mikf/gallery-dl/commit/9b21d3f13cfe69d461d3f57e97309bf23ef4066c and https://github.com/mikf/gallery-dl/commit/0dedbe759c67381c388a4d3951380d0359118139, which add the `--filter` and `--chapter-filter` cmdline options. These allow users to specify their own Python expressions to filter out images for which the expression evaluates to `False`. The available variable/keyword names for a specific URL can be looked up using `-K` or `-j`.

For example, to only download chapter 3 to 5 in English:
`gallery-dl --chapter-filter "lang == 'en' and 3 <= chapter <= 5" https://bato.to/comic/_/comics/fury-r12548`

These user-specified Python expression are currently evaluated using the builtin [eval](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#eval) function, which is generally considered unsafe for input from untrusted sources ([Eval really is dangerous](https://nedbatchelder.com/blog/201206/eval_really_is_dangerous.html)), but that shouldn't be an issue for this particular use-case (unless someone has a good argument against using it). On the plus side: `eval` is a lot faster and more powerful then for example youtube-dl's `--match-filter`, but there are probably good reasons why youtube-dl doesn't use `eval` for their filter implementation.

Todo:
- [x] Implement `--filter` and `--chapter-filter`
- [x] Provide manga-chapter metadata
- [x] Decide on sensible `globals` for filter expressions

issue_title: "incomplete" files passed to melody.evaluate should pass a warning.
issue_body:
f0 annotations are often output by algorithms with the "unvoiced" frames omitted, resulting in an incomplete set of time stamps. I was evaluating one of these types of melody output against a ground truth (with all time stamps, f0=0 for unvoiced frames), and melody.evaluate runs without problems.

After a brief talk with @justinsalamon, I learned that melody.evaluate is actually interpolating the missing f0 values. I can see use cases where you would want this behavior, but I might not have caught this problem. So, maybe melody.evaluate should pass a warning if a file with unevenly spaced time stamps is inputted, or, better yet, have a parameter to "fill missing frames".

Example files can be found [here](https://gist.github.com/rabitt/c45b969adf927cbe14b2)

issue_title: Use remote_tunnel only for local Docker registries

In situations when using custom registry with `PullDockerTasks`, `remote_tunnel` can take port which already used by some of containers. Therefore, `PullDockerTasks` should detect if `remote_tunnel` is really necessary.

In most cases `remote_tunnel` is necessary only for registries which point to the local host.

issue_title: Support for the vlan action for TC
issue_body:
Hello,

The vlan action for TC filters is not currently supported. I have added support for this. The file is very short so I pasted it here. It comes as pyroute2/netlink/rtnl/tcmsg/act_vlan.py and need to be imported from pyroute2/netlink/rtnl/tcmsg/common_act.py  . The plugins dictionnary needs to be extended to to include this new plugin. Could you please give some feedbacks on this ?

Thank you!

**pyroute2/netlink/rtnl/tcmsg/act_vlan.py**

`from pyroute2.netlink import nla
from pyroute2.netlink.rtnl.tcmsg.common import tc_actions
from socket import htons

v_actions = {'pop': 1,
             'push': 2,
             'modify': 3}


class options(nla):
    nla_map = (('TCA_VLAN_UNSPEC','none'),
               ('TCA_VLAN_TM','none'),
               ('TCA_VLAN_PARMS','tca_vlan_parms'),
               ('TCA_VLAN_PUSH_VLAN_ID','uint16'),
               ('TCA_VLAN_PUSH_VLAN_PROTOCOL','uint16'),
               ('TCA_VLAN_PAD','none'),
               ('TCA_VLAN_PUSH_VLAN_PRIORITY','uint8'))

    class tca_vlan_parms(nla):
        fields = (('index', 'I'),
                  ('capab', 'I'),
                  ('action', 'i'),
                  ('refcnt', 'i'),
                  ('bindcnt', 'i'),
                  ('v_action','i'),)


def get_parameters(kwarg):
    ret = {'attrs': []}
    parms = { 'v_action': v_actions[kwarg['v_action']]}
    parms['action'] = tc_actions[kwarg.get('action', 'pipe')]
    ret['attrs'].append(['TCA_VLAN_PARMS', parms])
    #Vlan id compulsory for "push" and "modify"
    if kwarg['v_action'] in ['push','modify']:
        ret['attrs'].append(['TCA_VLAN_PUSH_VLAN_ID', kwarg['id']])
    if 'priority' in kwarg:
        ret['attrs'].append(['TCA_VLAN_PUSH_VLAN_PRIORITY', kwarg['priority']])
    if kwarg.get('protocol','802.1Q') == '802.1ad':
        ret['attrs'].append(['TCA_VLAN_PUSH_VLAN_PROTOCOL', htons(0x88a8)])
    else:
        ret['attrs'].append(['TCA_VLAN_PUSH_VLAN_PROTOCOL', htons(0x8100)])
    return ret
`

The beginning of the common_act.py file looks like this now : 
**pyroute2/netlink/rtnl/tcmsg/common_act.py**
`
from pyroute2.netlink.rtnl.tcmsg import act_gact
from pyroute2.netlink.rtnl.tcmsg import act_bpf

plugins = {'gact': act_gact,
           'bpf': act_bpf,
           'police': act_police,
           'mirred': act_mirred,
           'connmark': act_connmark,
           'vlan': act_vlan,
           }


class nla_plus_tca_act_opt(object):
`

issue_title: has_role() function only recognizes roles by name.
issue_body: Currently, has_role() within commands > core.py only recognizes roles by their name as a string. This allows a user to create their own discord server and add a duplicate role which then allows the bot to have the same privileges on the original server thereby gaining a privilege escalation. In order to prevent this, has_role() should also accept role ID as an argument as the role IDs would be different between servers.

issue_title: Enable docker plugin on Windows
issue_body:
Add docker information on glance run on windows

#### Versions

Glances v2.8 with psutil v5.0.1
Distributor ID: Windows 10
Description:  Microsoft Windows 10 Professional 64bits
Release: 10

Docker 1.13

#### Logs
On windows, we have not information on docker container
It's possible that get information on windows. On Docker for Windows, clients can connect to the Docker Engine through a named pipe: npipe:////./pipe/docker_engine, or TCP socket at this URL: http://localhost:2375. (https://docs.docker.com/docker-for-windows/faqs/)

I propose to amend  glances/glances/plugins/glances_docker.py , in function _connect_old

`if WINDOWS:
                url = 'npipe:////./pipe/docker_engine'
            else:
                url = 'unix://var/run/docker.sock'
            if version is None:
                ret = init_docker(base_url=url)
            else:
                ret = init_docker(base_url=url,
                                  version=version)`

I generate soon a pull request

issue_title: nested resources
issue_body:
I run on this from time to time. In most cases nested resource can be handled by using custom fields but they cannot be trivially validated/serialized/deserialized. Also those new fields classes cannot be reused as unique resource types so in some cases it would be useful to have support for nesting resource definitions.

I see two approaches:

0. wrapper field class (like `SerializerField`) that can work like a single field and delegates validation and (de)serialisation to inner serialised stored as a attribute. Pros: more explicit, allows both types of objects to have different apis. Cons: feels like more complex and also will require more coding to define API that uses nested resources.
0. make `BaseSerializer` a descendant of `BaseField` class.  This should also work but will require serialisers and fields to have the same api. I'm afraid that this will make auto-describing of API more painful. Pros: shorter API descriptions. Cons: fields and serialisers have slightly different purposes so such coupling may introduce difficulties in future.

issue_title: Partial P2SH Transaction proposal Or just the signature
issue_body:
I am trying to figure the best way to partially sign a multi-sig P2SH transaction and just return the signature.

It looked at  the test itest_sign_pay_to_script_multisig() to see if there was a way to modify that code to do it, but as far as I can tell it wants to solve for a completely signed transaction. 

``` python
    def testFullSignMultiSigP2SH(self):
        """
        Test using pycoin to sign p2sh
        """
        console.terse("{0}\n".format(self.testFullSignMultiSigP2SH.__doc__))

        from pycoin.key import Key
        from pycoin.tx import Tx, TxIn, TxOut, tx_utils
        from pycoin.tx.pay_to import ScriptMultisig
        from pycoin.tx.pay_to import (address_for_pay_to_script,
                                      build_hash160_lookup, build_p2sh_lookup)
        from pycoin.tx.TxOut import standard_tx_out_script

        N, M = 3, 3
        keys = [Key(secret_exponent=i) for i in range(1, M+2)]
        tx_in = TxIn.coinbase_tx_in(script=b'')
        underlying_script = ScriptMultisig(n=N, sec_keys=[key.sec() for key in keys[:M]]).script()
        address = address_for_pay_to_script(underlying_script)
        self.assertEqual(address, "39qEwuwyb2cAX38MFtrNzvq3KV9hSNov3q")
        script = standard_tx_out_script(address)
        tx_out = TxOut(1000000, script)
        tx1 = Tx(version=1, txs_in=[tx_in], txs_out=[tx_out])
        tx2 = tx_utils.create_tx(tx1.tx_outs_as_spendable(), [address])
        hash160_lookup = build_hash160_lookup(key.secret_exponent() for key in keys[:M])
        p2sh_lookup = build_p2sh_lookup([underlying_script])
        tx2.sign(hash160_lookup=hash160_lookup, p2sh_lookup=p2sh_lookup)
        self.assertEqual(tx2.bad_signature_count(), 0)

```

Any suggestions on how to use pycoin to just return a signature of a given p2sh transaction proposal?

issue_body:
A long time ago a [wrote a patch for libxslt](https://bugzilla.gnome.org/show_bug.cgi?id=612186) to write xslt evaluation profiles in gprof compatible format. This makes gprof2dot an awesome tool to analyze xxslt slowness. Unfortunately I had to apply a little patch to gprof2dot - see below. This seems to be caused by special events: TIME and TOTAL_TIME. Do you have any insight here what goes wrong? I've uploaded a sample profile to: https://gist.github.com/ensonic/d78b65fc3d9067b97f82

```patch
￼diff --git a/gprof2dot.py b/gprof2dot.py
index 7a11b3a..9a6e7b9 100755
--- a/gprof2dot.py
+++ b/gprof2dot.py
@@ -491,7 +491,12 @@ class Profile(Object):
             return function[outevent]
     
     def _integrate_call(self, call, outevent, inevent):
-        assert outevent not in call
+        # Do we have double edges, recursion? cycles should be handled below
+        if outevent not in call:
+            # outevent 'Total time' not in call, inevent 'Time'
+            sys.stderr.write ("outevent '%s' not in call, inevent '%s'\n" % (outevent.name, inevent.name))
+            return 0
+        #assert outevent not in call
         assert call.ratio is not None
         callee = self.functions[call.callee_id]
         subtotal = call.ratio *self._integrate_function(callee, outevent, inevent)
```

issue_title: `env` only works on linux-like systems
issue_body:
The fix for #162 uses `env -0`, which is a GNU extension that isn't part of POSIX, BSD, etc., which means a whole lot of things now break on OS X, FreeBSD, etc.

As far as I know, there is no perfect portable fix for #162; for example, the standard `env` command will produce the exact same output for these two cases:

    env a=foo b=bar
    env a='foo
    b=bar'

As far as I know, the only way to do it reliably and portably (at least to anything that's compatible with BSD3 or later, which is a lot more than just GNU…) from the shell is to first get a (potential) list of names by calling and parsing `env` or `printenv`, and then lookup each name one by one with `printenv`.

Of course that can obviously cause performance problems. You can solve that for GNU systems by trying `env -0` first and only falling back if that fails:

        env0 = session.run("env -0; echo")
        if env0[0] == 0 and not env 0[2].rstrip():
            self._curr = dict(line.split('=', 1)
                              for line in env0[1].split('\x00')
                              if '=' in line)
        else:
            lines = self.remote._session.run("env; echo")[1].splitlines()
            split = (line.split('=', 1) for line in lines)
            keys = (line[0] for line in split if len(line)>1)
            runs = ((key, session.run('printenv "%s"; echo' % key))
                    for key in keys)
            self._curr = dict((key, run[1].rstrip('\n')) for (key, run) in runs
                              if run[0] == 0 and run[1].rstrip('\n')
                              and not run[2])

Alternatively, for platforms without `env -0`, you can use some heuristics on the output of `env`, which aren't perfect, but may be good enough for most users.

Your existing code already skips any line without a `=`; this will truncate variables with embedded newlines, and it will mean that `a='foo\nb=bar'` will give you a fake variable `b`, but maybe that's acceptable.

You can eliminate the first problem by treating any line that doesn't start with a valid name followed by `=` as a continuation of the previous line, as in pigay's suggested patch for #162.

But there's no way to eliminate the last problem. I don't *think* that's likely to fail too often by accident… but you might want to think about whether someone could engineer some exploit that takes advantage of it.

```
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/dist-packages/plumbum/commands/modifiers.py", line 189, in __rand__
    tee_to[fd].write(data.decode('utf-8'))
UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf-8' codec can't decode bytes in position 4094-4095: unexpected end of data
```
The offending plumbum code from modifiers.py:
```
183                     data = os.read(fd.fileno(), 4096)
184                     if not data:  # eof
185                         continue
186 
187                     # Python conveniently line-buffers stdout and stderr for
188                     # us, so all we need to do is write to them
189                     tee_to[fd].write(data.decode('utf-8'))
```

It seems like gcc is using some unicode characters and `os.read(fd, 4096)` is splitting a unicode character in half at the 4095th byte boundary.  I've attached a contrived example of a file that has a unicode character in this position to expose this bug. [unicode.txt](https://github.com/tomerfiliba/plumbum/files/56041/unicode.txt)

```
import os
fd = os.open('unicode.txt', os.O_RDWR)
os.read(fd, 4096).decode('utf-8')
```

issue_title: Support `--filter-pattern` option

Writing about AWS Logs CLI tools http://stackoverflow.com/a/33636237/346478 I have realized, that `awslogs` does not support `filter patterns`.

Adding filter patterns in the way `AWSCLI` option `--filter-pattern` is doing would be handy.


The method
[CloudWatchLogs.Client.filter_log_events](http://boto3.readthedocs.org/en/latest/reference/services/logs.html#CloudWatchLogs.Client.filter_log_events)
offers the `filterPattern` parameter for that.

issue_title: urisplit doesn't work on Windows
issue_body:
Windows-like URIs for local filesystem, such as like `c:\users\whatever` trip up `urlsplit()` -- it detects `c` as the scheme.

And then smart_open fails with "unknown URI scheme" of course.

Originally reported by: **Dmitry Zhukov (Bitbucket: [dzhukov89](http://bitbucket.org/dzhukov89), GitHub: [dzhukov89](http://github.com/dzhukov89))**

----------------------------------------

Hi all!

This using TornadoScheduler below doesn't work

```
#!python

import motor
import tornado
class MyClass(object):

    def __init__(self):
        self.db = motor.MotorClient()['my_db']
        self.my_col = self.db['my_collection']

    @tornado.gen.coroutine
    def doSmthng1(self):
        print('start1')
        yield self.my_col.insert({'test': '1'})
        print('finish1')

def main():
    my_class_item = MyClass()
    ioloop = tornado.ioloop.IOLoop.instance()
    scheduler = TornadoScheduler()

    job2 = scheduler.add_job(my_class_item.doSmthng1, 'cron', second='*/5', id="cron_job")
    scheduler.start()
    ioloop.start()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

When the job is start, I get an error:
```
#!python

ERROR:tornado.application:Exception in callback functools.partial(<function wrap.<locals>.null_wrapper at 0x7f4c5eb35d08>, <tornado.concurrent.Future object at 0x7f4c5eb31f28>)

    return fn(*args, **kwargs)
```

```
#!python
yield self.my_col.insert({'test': '1'})
```
What's wrong?

issue_body:
Hi,
I created my first Telegram chatbot and I deployed it on Heroku. 

It doesn't work. My program has these files:
``` tree
Procfile
requirements.txt
Magghy.py
magghybot.py
nltk.txt
runtime.txt
telegramtoken.txt
botusers.csv
conversations.yml (in folder named lang)
math_words.json (in folder named lang)
```
The logs are:

``` Log
State changed from crashed to starting
Starting process with command `python magghybot.py`
State changed from starting to up
State changed from up to crashed
Process exited with status 0
State changed from crashed to down
Starting process with command `python magghybot.py`
State changed from starting to up
State changed from up to crashed
State changed from crashed to starting
Process exited with status 0
...
```


In Procfile I wrote:
```
worker: python magghybot.py
```

In magghybot.py file the code is:
``` Python
import os
import os.path
import sys
from inspect import getsourcefile
from os.path import abspath
from chatterbot import ChatBot

class MagghyBot(object):
    
    def __init__(self, lang = "english"):
        self.language = lang
        self.chatbot = ChatBot(
            'MagghyBot',
            logic_adapters=[
            "chatterbot.logic.MathematicalEvaluation",
            "chatterbot.logic.TimeLogicAdapter",
            "chatterbot.logic.BestMatch"
            ],
            #input_adapter="chatterbot.input.VariableInputTypeAdapter",
            #output_adapter="chatterbot.output.OutputAdapter"
            trainer='chatterbot.trainers.ChatterBotCorpusTrainer'
        ) 
        self.instdir = "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.5/lib/python3.5/site-packages/chatterbot_corpus/data" + self.language + "/"
        self.localdir = os.path.abspath(os.path.dirname(sys.argv[0])) + "/lang/" + self.language + "/chatcorpus/"

    def train(self):
        if self.checkdirnotempty(self.localdir):
            print(self.localdir)
            self.chatbot.train(
                self.localdir
            )
        elif self.checkdirnotempty(self.instdir):
            print(self.instdir)
            self.chatbot.train(
                self.instdir
            )
        else:
            print("Using standard english corpus")
            self.chatbot.train("chatterbot.corpus.english.greetings")
    
    def reply(self, phrase = ""):
        # Get a response to an input statement
        response = self.chatbot.get_response(phrase)
        return response
    
    def checkdirnotempty(self, folder = ""):
        check = False
        if os.path.isdir(folder):
            entities = os.listdir(folder)
            for entity in entities:
                if os.path.isfile(folder + entity):
                    check = True
                    break
        return check`
```
In Magghy.py, I wrote:
``` Python
import time
import random
import datetime
import telepot
import os
import sys
import subprocess
from magghybot import MagghyBot

telegramtoken = '' #telegram bot token from BotFather
checkuserid = 394287240 #enable users whitelist, so only certain people can talk with this bot
usersfile = 'botusers' #the file where we store the list of users who can talk with bot
attemptsfile = '/tmp/attempts.log' #the file where we log denied accesses
active = 1 #if set to 0 the bot will stop

language = "italiano"

chatter = MagghyBot(language)
chatter.train()

if telegramtoken == '' and os.path.isfile("telegramtoken.txt"):
    text_file = open("telegramtoken.txt", "r")
    telegramtoken = text_file.read().replace("\n", "")
    text_file.close()

print("Connecting to Telegram...")
bot = telepot.Bot(telegramtoken)
print(bot.getMe())

def listusers():
    if not os.path.isfile(usersfile):
        return ''
    text_file = open(usersfile, "r")
    lines = text_file.read().split(',')
    text_file.close()
    del lines[-1] #remove last element since it is blank
    return lines

def adduser(name):
    csv = ""
    users = listusers()
    if users != "":
        for usr in users:
            csv = csv+usr+","
    csv = csv+name+","
    text_file = open(usersfile, "w")
    text_file.write(csv)
    text_file.close()
    
def deluser(name):
    csv = ""
    users = listusers()
    if users != "":
        for usr in users:
            if usr != name:
                csv = csv+usr+","
    text_file = open(usersfile, "w")
    text_file.write(csv)
    text_file.close()

def handle(msg):
    global bot
    global chatter
    global language
    
    chat_id = msg['chat']['id']
    sender = msg['from']['id']

    users = listusers()


    if checkuserid == 1:
        verified = 0
        if users != "":
            for usr in users:
                if str(sender) == usr:
                    verified = 1
        if verified == 0:
            bot.sendMessage(chat_id, "I don't talk with strangers, dear "+str(sender))
            #write this user in the list of attempted accesses
            if attemptsfile != '':
                lines = ''
                if os.path.isfile(attemptsfile):
                    text_file = open(attemptsfile, "r")
                    lines = text_file.read()
                    text_file.close()
                lines = lines + str(datetime.datetime.now()) + " --- UserdID: " + str(sender) + " DENIED \n"
                text_file = open(attemptsfile, "w")
                text_file.write(lines)
                text_file.close()
            return
    
    command = ''
    
        
    try:
        if msg['text'] != '':
            command = msg['text']
            print('Got command: ' + command)
    except:
        print("No text in this message")
        

    if command == '/time':
        bot.sendMessage(chat_id, str(datetime.datetime.now()))
    elif '/adduser' in command:
        if len(command.split(' ')) > 1:
            usrname = command.split(' ')[1]
            adduser(usrname)
            bot.sendMessage(chat_id, "User "+usrname+" added")
    elif '/deluser' in command:
        if len(command.split(' ')) > 1:
            usrname = command.split(' ')[1]
            deluser(usrname)
            bot.sendMessage(chat_id, "User "+usrname+" deleted")
    elif command == '/help':
        bot.sendMessage(chat_id, "/adduser /deluser /time /exit")
    elif command == '/exit':
        global active
        active = False
        bot.sendMessage(chat_id, "The bot will shutdown in 10 seconds")
    elif command != '':
        answer = chatter.reply(command)
        bot.sendMessage(chat_id, str(answer))



bot.message_loop(handle)
print('I am listening ...')

while active:
    time.sleep(10)
print("Exiting")
sys.exit()`
```

What is the problem? Please help me!

Thanks a lot!

Adri

issue_title: Support for Python 2.6 and 3.3 should be dropped
issue_body:
Supporting outdated Python versions hinders development, because you have to worry about compatibility with obsolete and insecure software.

https://devguide.python.org/#status-of-python-branches

- 2.6: end-of-life 2013-10-29
- 3.3: end-of-life 2017-09-29

As can be seen above, 2.6 was put to rest more than 4 years ago, and 3.3 almost half a year ago. EOL means no updates -- even security updates. Using these Python versions is a really bad idea anyway, so why even test for them?

issue_title: Crash in utils.py in user_or_ip with Django 2.0
issue_body:
utils.py l.28 uses `user.is_authenticated()` which is [deprecated in Django 1.10](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/2.0/releases/1.10/#using-user-is-authenticated-and-user-is-anonymous-as-methods) and [removed in 2.0](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/2.0/releases/2.0/#features-removed-in-2-0),
raising at TypeError exception ('bool' object is not callable).
The function has been replaced with the corresponding property `user.is_authenticated`

```python
def user_or_ip(request):
    if request.user.is_authenticated(): # Removed in Django 2.0
        return str(request.user.pk)
    return request.META['REMOTE_ADDR']
```

Since Django 2.0:

> Using User.is_authenticated() and User.is_anonymous() as methods rather than properties is no longer supported.

issue_title: StPipe and table reload from outside
issue_body:
First of all, thanks for the hard work, the simplicity & efficiency of your smart-table, I very much like most of the paradigms.

I've searched for past issues for my problem, but couldn't find my exact scenario, nor any sense of something that I might not have "gotten" from your philosophy with smart-table, so here is my issue.

I'm using your basic pipe/ajax example (with pagination), and things are working fine. I have no specific code to provide, as my code is exactly like your example of a custom pipe function loading data from a server.

In the case of your example, you handle the "initial/first load of data" case using the linking function of your stPipe directive (on post link, call ctrl.pipe()). 

But this way of handling things works fine only for the initial creation of the directive : afterwards, I need to reload the table based on external changes (a user profile change in my application causes the data to be outdated so I want to reload the table / call the pipe function again). 

I have the option to reload my entire state upon this user profile change, but that's far from perfect (other elements in my view do not need to be reloaded, only the table).

I've tried to call my custom pipe function from outside the directive, but this way the tableState.pagination.numberOfPages is not provided, thus not updated, causing a UI bug.

I could also re-set the pipe function to force the intial post link function to execute again, but that's not ideal either.

I'm considering 2 options : 

- modifying your stPagination directive to be able to do 2 way data-binding onto the numberOfPages attribute, so that I can update it from the outside when calling the pipe function without needing to pass-in the tableState Parameter. 

- providing a reload() method from the directive to the outside world, method that would then have access internally to the tableState, and call the custom pipe function with this tableState. This pretty much sums up to the ng-table table.reload() feature.

What are you thoughts on the problem ? I I do a pull request, would you consider my proposition ?

issue_title: open redirect vulnerability
issue_body:
We discovered recently while experimenting that a site using django-saml2-auth to authenticate against Okta is vulnerable to an [open redirect vulnerability](https://blog.qualys.com/securitylabs/2016/01/07/open-redirection-a-simple-vulnerability-threatens-your-web-applications). For example, a maliciously crafted URL like `https://safe.site/accounts/login_okta?next=//evil.site` will redirect the user to Okta for authentication, which will then redirect them to `evil.site` after they successfully authenticate. The evil site operator can then engage in a phishing attack, or even take advantage of the user's `safe.site` credentials to compromise the original site.

I have a patch to close this vulnerability and will submit a PR shortly.

issue_title: URL Regex patterns are ignored
issue_body:
When reversing an overridden URL pattern that use a regex, the regexes are ignored and the last URL is used.  

```
urlpatterns = [
    url(r'^word/(?P<b>\w+)/?$', views.call_word, name='smart_url'),
    url(r'^number/(?P<a>\d+)/?$', views.call_number, name='smart_url'),
]
```

If I call this in Django, the regex is respected and only one URL will match:
`reverse('smart_url', args=['abc'])` results in `word/abc`
`reverse('smart_url', args=[123])` results in `number/123`

In Javascript the regex is overlooked and the last URL with a matching name and number of arguments is used:

`Urls.smart_url('abc')` results in `number/abc` 
`Urls.smart_url(123)`  results in `number/123`

issue_title: Read matlab struct members into a python dict instead of numpy structured array
issue_body:
I ran into this bug in numpy when reading in a .mat that has a struct containing huge matrices. (the dtwhole dtype was > 2GB)

https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/6235

I was able to work around this bug by returning the python dict `struct_data` early instead of trying to convert it to a numpy structure:

```
diff --git a/hdf5storage/Marshallers.py b/hdf5storage/Marshallers.py
index a37fbbf..74a363c 100644
--- a/hdf5storage/Marshallers.py
+++ b/hdf5storage/Marshallers.py
@@ -1079,6 +1079,9 @@ class NumpyScalarArrayMarshaller(TypeMarshaller):
                 except:
                     pass

+            if matlab_class == 'struct':
+                return struct_data
+
             # If it isn't multi element, we need to pack all the values
             # in struct_array inside of numpy.object_'s so that the code
             # after this that depends on this will work.
```

Is there any disadvantage to doing this? Could this be made the default or an option flag?

> You can tell Requests to stop waiting for a response after a given number of seconds with the `timeout` parameter. Nearly all production code should use this parameter in nearly all requests. Failure to do so can cause your program to hang indefinitely.

issue_title: Common __init__ signature for all backends
issue_body:
All backends should be compatible at least partly with the signature of django.core.files.storage.FileSystemStorage:
```python
def __init__(self, location=None, base_url=None, file_permissions_mode=None, directory_permissions_mode=None):
```
Not sure about file and directory permission modes but location and base_url can surely be used in all backends. *location* should be a path (e.g. "<dir>/<dir>/<dir>") while *base_url* could provide the rest of URL for connection (protocol + hostname + port). For some backends even authentication credentials could be extracted from *base_url*.
Currently we have SFTP backend which has no __init__ args, then we have FTP backend which needs full URL in *location* arg and the rest of backends are not very consistent as well, I guess.
The purpose of this would be to make all backends interchangable without having to fiddle with settings too much.

issue_title: KM Late Entry
issue_body:
While fitting a Kaplan Meier with late entries for some simulations, I noticed an unexpected behavior. When late entries and event times occur at the same point (ex. t = 1), the late entries count towards the denominator. This is different from R and can lead to a misleading result. Example)

`
import pandas as pd 
import numpy as np 
from lifelines import KaplanMeierFitter

np.random.seed(101)

n = 10000
df = pd.DataFrame()
df['id'] = [i for i in range(n)]
df['t'] = np.ceil(np.random.weibull(1,size=n)*5)
df['cens'] = np.ceil(np.random.weibull(1,size=n)*3)
df['e'] = np.floor(np.random.weibull(1.5,size=n)*2)
df['ft'] = 10
df['obs_t'] = np.min(df[['t','cens','ft']],axis=1).astype(int)
df['d'] = (np.where(df['t']<=Ct,1,0)) * (np.where(df['t']<=df['cens'],1,0))
df['c'] = (np.where(df['cens']<=Ct,1,0)) * (np.where(df['cens']<df['t'],1,0))
df['y'] = (np.where(df['t']>df['e'],1,0)) * (np.where(df['cens']>df['e'],1,0)) * (np.where(Ct > df['e'],1,0))
df = df[['id','t','cens','ft','e','obs_t','c','d','y']].rename(columns={'e':'t_enter','obs_t':'t_out'})
dfo = df.loc[df['y']==1].copy() #"observed data"
rf = pd.DataFrame()

#Fitting KM to full data
km1 = KaplanMeierFitter()
km1.fit(df['t_out'],event_observed=df['d'])
rf['KM_true'] = km1.survival_function_

#Fitting KM to "observed" data 
km2 = KaplanMeierFitter()
km2.fit(dfo['t_out'],entry=dfo['t_enter'],event_observed=dfo['d'])
rf['KM_lifelines0.14.6'] = km2.survival_function_

#Version of KM where late entries occur after
rf['KM_lateenterafter'] = np.cumprod(1 - (km2.event_table.observed/(km2.event_table.at_risk - km2.event_table.entrance)))
print(rf)
`
Which gives
`
     KM_true  KM_lifelines0.14.6  KM_lateenterafter
0   1.000000            1.000000                NaN
1   0.818600            0.885067           0.812788
2   0.674796            0.756820           0.672143
3   0.552316            0.627204           0.548928
4   0.458322            0.520323           0.452635
5   0.375165            0.428398           0.372124
6   0.304956            0.348610           0.302718
7   0.253890            0.290921           0.252623
8   0.211575            0.242434           0.210519
9   0.174656            0.200130           0.173784
10  0.148643            0.170323           0.147901
`
My solution is a bit ugly with the NaN, but that column is the behavior I would expect. This probably went unnoticed since most data often has few tied event/entry times

issue_title: Bug: helper.singleresult calls len on filter objects
issue_body: Python 3 functions like `map` and `filter` return iterator objects, not `list` objects like in python 2. The `helper.singleresult` function accepts a generic object, `items`, and calls `len(items)` to check the length. This can fail in python 3, as `items` can often be `filter` or `map` objects with no `__len__` method defined.

issue_title: How to include translated model when using select_related?
issue_body:
I'm trying to use `select_related` on some  translated models in order to reduce the number of queries needed when loading a page on my site.

I was able to get `select_related` working (see mstarinc/django-hvad@cf66fec33038162b26a6d3cc92a7dabcd3f690df ), but  it only selects the shared model across relationships, and doesn't include related translated models. So when I later try to access translated fields across a relation, it generates a new query for each object.

Would someone mind commenting on my commit and giving me some guidance as to what I will have to change to get this fully working? 


Here's the setup I have:

```
class Location(TranslatableModel):
    """ A location (e.g. a venue where an event is held. """
    city = models.CharField(max_length=30, blank=True)
    country = models.CharField(max_length=2, blank=True, choices=common_fields.COUNTRY_CHOICES)
   
    translations = TranslatedFields(
        name = models.CharField(max_length=128, blank=True),
    )

class Event(TranslatableModel):
    event_type = models.ForeignKey(EventType, blank=True, null=True)
    date = models.DateField()
    location = models.ForeignKey(Location, blank=True, null=True)
    
    translations = TranslatedFields(
        name = models.CharField(max_length=128),
        description = models.TextField(blank=True, null=True),
    )
```

What I want to do is 

```
all_events = Event.objects.language().select_related('event_type', 'location').all()
```

After I modified django-hvad, the above query works fine and properly retrieves the related objects, but the `Location` objects are untranslated, so if I do something like `[e.location.name for e in all_events]`, it generates an extra query for every `Event` object. If I access 'location.name' using `safe_translation_getter`, it returns `None`, and if I use `lazy_translation_getter`, it generates 3 queries per `Event` object, which is even worse (2 existence queries, and one to get the name).

issue_title: /convert-token/ is giving ValidationError on email field.
Autobahn handshake timeout
issue_body:
We are currently having issues in our development environment where our database is on a remote machine. The Autobahn ```openHandshakeTimeout``` is set to 5 by default, and with our remote database + a bunch of authentication processing it frequently takes between 5 and 10 seconds to finish the handshake. This is causing the connection to timeout. 

Changing the timeout value passed to ```self.ws_factory.setProtocolOptions``` in daphne/http_protocol.py to 10 seconds fixes this issue and stops Autobahn from disconnecting.

e.g.
```python
self.ws_factory.setProtocolOptions(
        autoPingTimeout=ping_timeout,
        allowNullOrigin=True,
        openHandshakeTimeout=ws_open_handshake_timeout
    )
```

I have made a change to accept a new parameter ```--ws_open_handshake_timeout``` in runserver to pass this parameter through to http_protocol.py - should I submit a pull request for this and if so is this new parameter necessary, or should it be using the ```websocket_connect_timeout``` parameter and then add ```--websocket_connect_timeout``` as an argument to runserver in Channels?

issue_title: use 'in' keyword as variable name leads to invalid syntax error
issue_body:
Got the error when pip install -U on the  latest version:
```
Running setup.py install for facebookads
    File "C:\Python27\Lib\site-packages\facebookads\adobjects\adruleexecutionoptions.py", line 47
      in = 'IN'
      ^
SyntaxError: invalid syntax
```
And the class definition is here:
```python
class AdRuleExecutionOptions(
    AbstractObject,
):

    
def __init__(self, api=None):
        super(AdRuleExecutionOptions, self).__init__()
        self._isAdRuleExecutionOptions = True
        self._api = api

    class Field(AbstractObject.Field):
        field = 'field'
        operator = 'operator'
        value = 'value'

    class Operator:
        equal = 'EQUAL'
        in = 'IN'

    _field_types = {
        'field': 'string',
        'operator': 'Operator',
        'value': 'Object',
    }

    @classmethod
    def _get_field_enum_info(cls):
        field_enum_info = {}
        field_enum_info['Operator'] = AdRuleExecutionOptions.Operator.__dict__.values()
        return field_enum_info
```

Hello,

I currently facing an issue plone.app.widget and I am not sure if I have to change the field definition in my content type (dexterity) or the problem comes from somewhere else:

plone.app.widget 1.7.1
plone.formwidget.autocomplete 1.2.7
plone 4.3.4

This is my field:

    selectedwines = RelationList(
        title=u"MyChoice",
        value_type=RelationChoice(
            title=_(u"Related"),
            source=PathSourceBinder(portal_type='Image')),

issue_title: view.thumb_size() shouldn't be called on that objects that are not accessible to a user

issue_title: PyZmq with PGM on Ubuntu

Neither Ubuntu's `python3-zmq` nor PyPi's `pyzmq` seem to be built with the `--with-pgm` option, giving `zmq.error.ZMQError: Protocol not supported` on instantiating a PGM socket.

The suggested approach is to build everything from source. So I Installed OpenPGM from Ubuntu package, and installed `libzmq-4.2.2` with `./configure --with-pgm --prefix SOMEPATH` followed by a `make install`. After several tries, this seemed to work.

Next I downloaded `pyzmq-14.7.0` and ran `python3 setup.py build --zmq=SOMEPATH`.

issue_title: dns names can't include underscores
issue_body:
This gives an `invalid_argument` error on my system...

    import sys
    import zmq

    context = zmq.Context()
    socket = context.socket(zmq.SUB)

    socket.connect (b"tcp://joo_joo:10050")

...even if `joo_joo` is mapped to localhost in `/etc/hosts`.  Underscores are indeed forbidden for top-level domain names, but they can definitely be found in subdomains and local network configurations.  

The equivalent code in C++ runs with no difficulty, so I think it's somewhere in pyzmq.

issue_title: Use libdeepspeech in DeepSpeech.py and remove duplicate code
issue_body: Part of libdeepspeech is a native implementation of the processing done in audiofile_to_input_vector (as well as the reimplementation of python_speech_features). We should use the Python bindings for this library instead, partly because it's considerably faster than the equivalent Python/python_speech_features code, but mostly so we don't have two copies of the same thing which could possibly diverge.

issue_title: Session is lost when Neo4j is restarted
issue_body:
With version 1.0.2, the driver does not reconnect when the server closes its connection. This is an important issue because we have to reinitialize the session if we restart the Neo4j process.

Is it possible to create a new Bolt connection when the current one is lost?

The issue can be reproduced with the following script:

```python
import os, socket, time
from neo4j.v1 import GraphDatabase, basic_auth

driver = GraphDatabase.driver("bolt://localhost", auth=basic_auth("neo4j", "neo4j"))
session = driver.session()

# 1) Execute a query

for record in session.run("RETURN 1"):
  print("### Got a record; Restart Neo4j")

# 2) Restart Neo4j

os.system("./neo4j-community-3.0.6/bin/neo4j restart")

# 3) When Neo4j is ready, execute the next query

while True:
  try:
    socket.create_connection(("localhost", 7687)).close()
    break
  except socket.error:
    time.sleep(0.3)

print("### Bolt is ready to be used; driver should be able to reconnect")

for record in session.run("RETURN 1"):
  print("### It works!")

session.close()
```

This script produces the following output:

```
### Got a record; Restart Neo4j
Stopping Neo4j.. stopped
Starting Neo4j.
Started neo4j (pid 7595). By default, it is available at http://localhost:7474/
There may be a short delay until the server is ready.
See /h/neo4j-community-3.0.6/logs/neo4j.log for current status.
### Bolt is ready to be used; driver should be able to reconnect
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test.py", line 27, in <module>
    for record in session.run("RETURN 1"):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/neo4j/v1/session.py", line 232, in __iter__
    self.connection.fetch()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/neo4j/v1/connection.py", line 311, in fetch
    raw.writelines(self.channel.chunk_reader())
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/neo4j/v1/connection.py", line 169, in chunk_reader
    chunk_header = self._recv(2)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/neo4j/v1/connection.py", line 152, in _recv
    raise ProtocolError("Server closed connection")
neo4j.v1.exceptions.ProtocolError: Server closed connection
```

```
            "DEPRECATED: Manage Jupyter kernel specifications."
```

Initialize subcommand in traitlets though does not print it:

```
No subcommand specified. Must specify one of: ['install-self', 'list', 'install']

Subcommands

Subcommands are launched as `jupyter kernelspec cmd [args]`. For information on

    [DEPRECATED] Install the IPython kernel spec directory for this Python.
```

It default to the subapp description, which in itself is not deprecated, But its IPython alias is. 

Does affect many subapp.

issue_title: Build option -march=native causes unexpected issue
issue_body:
Hello,

We have spent some time debugging a runtime issue with annoy: our service was generating core dumps in cloud environment but not on developer envs, triggered by "illegal instructions". What happened is setup.py specifies `-march=native` and our images were built using a cloud CI service, which node architectures was a superset of our deployment nodes one (no avx2, fma, etc.).

Our solution was to drop the `-march=native` option from setup.py.

I do not know if you want to do anything about that. I am just suggesting a more conservative build option choice may avoid weird runtime issues, at the price of performance (though we have not compared both). Maybe documenting it would be enough.

Feel free to close the issue without resolution, and thank you for annoy.

issue_title: Build RPMs using mock inside a Docker container
issue_body:
Our Jenkins is based on Ubuntu and Ubuntu always provides an old (and not very reliable) version of `mock`.

We should move the build of RPMs inside a Docker container (based on Fedora) which then runs mock.
As an example on how it is possible see https://daniele.vigano.me/git/dani/qgis3-cd-copr/src/branch/master/docker/Dockerfile.f27 and https://daniele.vigano.me/git/dani/qgis3-cd-copr/src/branch/master/buildbot/master.cfg#L26.

This will also make the Jenkins job fully portable on any Linux+Docker capable system (i.e. a Linux laptop).

issue_title: Let the dbserver listening on localhost too even if IP is set
issue_body:
Currently in `openquake.cfg` we have a single config parameter called `host` for the dbserver: this is used for both the dbserver IP (where to push messages) and as the IP where to listen at. On a single machine it's left to `localhost` which is fine. On a cluster the master node IP must be set instead: on master this has the side effect that dbserver is not available on `localhost` but only on the iface associated to that IP.

My proposal is not to change the `openquake.cfg` (which would mean split `host` into `host` and `bind_address`) but, when host != localhost, is to bind dbserver on the defined IP _and_ localhost.

issue_title: Remove plone.i18n dependency
issue_body:
We require `plone.i18n` for some statistics. On the master branch, this package requires `Products.CMFPlone`. See [this commit](https://github.com/plone/plone.i18n/commit/0e49ae551e91de3a7190c253ffe7986879cc19e5). We don't want to pull in all of Plone.

We only use `plone.i18n` in [two lines](https://github.com/collective/i18ndude/blob/4.2/src/i18ndude/visualisation.py#L141-L142). The [`getLanguages` function](https://github.com/plone/plone.i18n/blob/3.0.7/plone/i18n/locales/languages.py#L22-L28) that we call, simply delivers a list of languages. I don't think this list changes much between Plone versions. We could copy what we need into `i18ndude` itself.

We can still try the import, and if it fails, we use our own list.

Maybe what we need (seemingly only a mapping between say 'nl' and 'Dutch') is in a small other package that we could use.

issue_title: Specify checkout target (tag, branch) when running
issue_body:
I'm having an issue with pulling a repo, and I'm pretty sure it has to do with me using a new version of either GitPython or Git (it worked previously).

Here's the relevant snippet:

```python
# Attempt to read from repo on filesystem
repo = Repo(repo_dir)

# Trigger re-create if repository is bare
if repo.bare:
    raise git.exc.InvalidGitRepositoryError

# Set origin
origin = repo.remotes.origin
origin.pull()
```

In this case, I am trying to check out a git tag that already exists, and I want to run a "pull" to ensure I have the latest changes from that target. As I mentioned, it was working previously - but something changed, causing the following stack trace (which originates from that last line in the above example):

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/<project>.py", line 44, in setup_repo
    origin.pull()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/git/remote.py", line 709, in pull
    res = self._get_fetch_info_from_stderr(proc, progress)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/git/remote.py", line 587, in _get_fetch_info_from_stderr
    finalize_process(proc)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/git/util.py", line 155, in finalize_process
    proc.wait(**kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/git/cmd.py", line 326, in wait
    raise GitCommandError(self.args, status, errstr)
GitCommandError: 'git pull -v origin' returned with exit code 1
```

Naturally, I ran the same command in that repo and get the same result:

```
vagrant@pythondev:/tmp/repo_dir/v0.0.0.26$ git pull -v origin
From < git remote >
 = [up to date]      master     -> origin/master
You asked to pull from the remote 'origin', but did not specify
a branch. Because this is not the default configured remote
for your current branch, you must specify a branch on the command line.
```

As provided in the error message, I just need to specify the tag like so: `git pull -v origin v0.0.0.26`. However, upon looking at what I believe is the [source code behind the GitPython `.pull()` function](https://github.com/gitpython-developers/GitPython/blob/9bebaca85a19e0ac8a776ee09981f0c826e1cafa/git/remote.py#L745), it's not clear if I am able to specify this argument to that function.

So, in summary, is there a way to specify the branch or tag that I wish to pull when I use the `.pull()` function? Thanks in advance!

issue_title: Fastest way to get the latest commit?
issue_body:
I'm trying to get the latest commit for each item in a list of `Tree` or `Blob` objects (sth. like a repo browser). Currently i'm doing something like

    repo = git.Repo("test.git")
    tree = repo.tree()
    for obj in tree:
    	print obj, obj.path, repo.iter_commits(paths=obj.path, max_count=1).next()

but this is incredible slow. Another solution is to use `repo.iter_commits()` to fill a dict of path <-> commit:

    for commit in repo.iter_commits(paths=paths):
        for f in commit.stats.files.keys():
            p = f[:f.index('/')] if '/' in f else f
            if p in latest_commits:
                continue

            latest_commits[p] = commit

However, in the worst case this one iterates over *all* commits in the entire repository (which is obviously a very bad idea).

Is there a faster solution?

issue_title: Some Vertica 'UNION ALL' queries need brackets
issue_body:
Currently PyPika creates union queries as `query1 UNION ALL query2`.

This works fine in most cases, e.g. 

```
SELECT id FROM myTable
UNION ALL
SELECT id FROM myTable
```

At least for Vertica though, there are some queries where this throws an error, e.g.:

```
SELECT id FROM myTable ORDER BY id ASC
UNION ALL
SELECT id FROM myTable ORDER BY id ASC
```

The fix is as simple as adding brackets:

```
(SELECT id FROM myTable ORDER BY id ASC)
UNION ALL
(SELECT id FROM myTable ORDER BY id ASC)
```

issue_body:
I am using two separate access keys in my project: one which has access to my media bucket and one which has access to SES.

When I have both keys set in my project, I get a traceback.
```
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID = config.get("s3_storage", "aws_access_key_id")
AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY = config.get("s3_storage", "aws_secret_access_key")

# DJANGO SES
```

> botocore.exceptions.ClientError: An error occurred (AccessDenied) when calling the SendRawEmail operation: User '{my_bucket_user_arn}' is not authorized to perform 'ses:SendRawEmail' on resource '{some_amazon_arn}:identity/no-reply@mydomain.com'
[14/Mar/2018 15:25:57] "POST /accounts/login/ HTTP/1.1" 500 136132

When I comment out 

```
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID = config.get("s3_storage", "aws_access_key_id")
AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY = config.get("s3_storage", "aws_secret_access_key")
```
everything works as expected.

I think this is happening because of the `__init__` method in `django_amazon_ses.py`, which just uses `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID` and `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` when they are supplied instead of first checking for `AWS_SES_ACCESS_KEY_ID` and `AWS_SES_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`.

```

            aws_access_key_id=access_key_id,

``` 
How do I make it use my SES_ACCESS_KEY instead of my regular ACCESS_KEY?

issue_title: "deblend_sources" properly deblends only the first blend it encounters
issue_body:
The deblending code in `deblend_sources` now properly deblends only the first blend it encounters, as it re-writes the reference to original `data` and therefore passes wrong image data to underlying `_deblend_source` function after first successful deblending. The following simple patch fixes it:

```diff
         if source_deblended.nlabels > 1:
             # replace the original source with the deblended source
             source_mask = (source_deblended.data > 0)
-            data = segm_deblended.data
-            data[source_slice][source_mask] = (
+            temp_data = segm_deblended.data
+            temp_data[source_slice][source_mask] = (
                 source_deblended.data[source_mask] + last_label)
-            segm_deblended.data = data    # needed to call data setter
+            segm_deblended.data = temp_data    # needed to call data setter
             last_label += source_deblended.nlabels
 
     if relabel:
```

I am just getting into scikit image and came across some weird issue. I want to load a bunch of images as an ImageCollection, which works just fine. However, all images are loaded as uint8, but I need to have all images in the collection to be uint16.

When I try to use the example code from http://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/api/skimage.io.html#skimage.io.ImageCollection:
```python
def imread_convert(f):

```
I get an error when trying to use the collection as normal, for example plot the data:

issue_title: No validation error is raised on PATCH with unknown attribute
issue_body:
With PUT, Cornice is in charge of validating against view schema. Works fine:

```
echo '{"title": "change"}' | http PUT "http://0.0.0.0:8888/v1/buckets/default/collections/articles/records/ff7db2ad-cc85-4715-b563-1579d4c27c3b" --auth=user:pass
HTTP/1.1 400 Bad Request
Access-Control-Expose-Headers: Backoff, Retry-After, Alert
Content-Length: 231
Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8
Date: Fri, 10 Jul 2015 10:45:42 GMT
Server: waitress

{
    "code": 400, 
    "details": [
        {
            "description": "data is missing", 
            "location": "body", 
            "name": "data"
        }, 
        {
            "description": "title is not allowed", 
            "location": "body", 
            "name": "title"
        }
    ], 
    "errno": 107, 
    "error": "Invalid parameters", 
    "message": "data is missing"
}
```

With PATCH, it does not raise:
```
echo '{"title": "change"}' | http PATCH "http://0.0.0.0:8888/v1/buckets/default/collections/articles/records/ff7db2ad-cc85-4715-b563-1579d4c27c3b" --auth=user:pass
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Access-Control-Expose-Headers: Backoff, Retry-After, Alert
Content-Length: 202
Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8
Date: Fri, 10 Jul 2015 10:47:45 GMT
Etag: "1436525081662"
Last-Modified: Fri, 10 Jul 2015 10:44:41 GMT
Server: waitress

{
    "data": {
        "id": "ff7db2ad-cc85-4715-b563-1579d4c27c3b", 
        "last_modified": 1436525081662, 
        "title": "toto"
    }, 
    "permissions": {
        "write": [
            "basicauth:631c2d625ee5726172cf67c6750de10a3e1a04bcd603bc9ad6d6b196fa8257a6"
        ]
    }
}
```

**Explanation**:

Since the PATCH request payload does not always match schema, Cliquet does define any schema for the PATCH endpoint.
Instead, it validates the result of merge (old record + changes) against the resource mapping (*which BTW duplicates some Cornice code to add colander errors to the response*).

But the resource mapping is not the same as the schema that was used by the ``ViewSet``. This should be fixed.

issue_title: Compute and store a hash of all records in a collection on record update.
issue_body:
To support records signing, it might be useful to be able to compute a hash of all the records, which could be then signed then by a trusted party, in order to ensure the changes have been signed by the trusted party.

It is possible to compute the hash when the collection updates (e.g. at the same time we compute the `last_modified` field) and make it available in the metadata fields of the collection.

issue_title: Allow a global CORS policy configuration
issue_body:
It would be nice if there was a way to configure a global CORS policy for all resources. Although the documentation shows how an external object can be used to set the `cors_policy` for each resource (http://cornice.readthedocs.org/en/latest/api.html), it still doesn't seem very DRY. I currently have a large number of resources defined throughout my code and I will have to modify each one in order to support CORS. By providing a single point of configuration for all resources - or even a global default configuration - I think this would make dealing with CORS much nicer.

Something like this could be used in `main()`:

```python
config.route_prefix = '/api'
config.cors_policy = {'origins': ('*',), 'max_age': 42, 'credentials': True}

# Alternatively
config.cors_default_policy = {'origins': ('*',), 'max_age': 42, 'credentials': True}
```

issue_title: Flags for RAIM availability / use in SPP and baselines output msgs
Do something different with NaNs / Infs in json logger
issue_body:
JSON doesn't, apparently, have a widely-supported means of encoding floating-point NaNs or infinities.  If Piski generates an SBP message containing one, presently json_logger.py (as used by the console and serial_link) will raise a RuntimeWarning and insert (the json.dumps of) an empty dict into the json output file.  This isn't ideal because
 - We might even want to use NaNs and inifnities to represent actual quantities.
 - Even if we try to avoid generating them, we'll still see them sometimes from bugs.  It'd be nice to know about it rather than the message just getting eaten before it makes it into the logfile.
 - The empty entry in the output, which lacks all the metadata fields like delta/timestamp/data/etc, breaks e.g. gnss_analysis:records2table.py

Maybe we could patch over all the NaNs in the message with some sentinel value, or zero, and set a flag field to label it as having had NaNs?  Or at least instead of inserting an empty message into the file, either don't insert one at all or insert a special one to mark that a whole message is missing due to NaNs.

cc @fnoble @mfine @mookerji

Rely on `MutableMapping` for `SmartDict`, `LazyDict` and `LaziestDict` and don't override the following methods: `__contains__`, `keys`, `items`, `values`, `get`, `__eq__`, `__ne__`, `pop`, `popitem`, `clear`, `update`, and `setdefault`.
* https://docs.python.org/2/library/collections.html#collections-abstract-base-classes
* https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/blob/c4d74fd3945567d43888b8e0e19154b27ea445a9/invenio/utils/datastructures.py

issue_title: i18n bundles still registered when the bluprint is not
issue_body:
## The issue

```Flask-Collect``` doesn't collect static folders from modules which don't have a registered blueprint.

In our case if the blueprint is not registered 
``` python
 if app.config['I18N_SET_LANGUAGE_URL'] \
           and app.config['I18N_LANGUAGES']:
            app.register_blueprint(
                blueprint, url_prefix=app.config['I18N_SET_LANGUAGE_URL'])
```
the bundle will still be registered on the ```invenio_assets.bundle``` entry point [2] and ```invenio-assets``` will try to build it causing the following error

```
IOError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '/home/travis/virtualenv/python2.7.9/var/instance/static/js/invenio_i18n/angularLangSelector.js'
```
[1] https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-i18n/blob/master/invenio_i18n/ext.py#L129-L132
[2] https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-i18n/blob/master/setup.py#L91-L93

issue_title: Example does not work
issue_body:
Starting from a freshly created virtualenv. I had to add several packages in `requirements.txt` and some have to be installed from git:
- pip install invenio-files-rest
- pip install -e git+https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-oauth2server.git#egg=invenio-oauth2server
- pip install invenio-indexer
- pip install invenio-jsonschemas
- pip install invenio_records_rest
- pip install invenio_records_ui
- pip install invenio_search_ui
- pip install invenio_records_ui
- pip install invenio_deposit
- pip install -e git+https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-deposit.git#egg=invenio-deposit

Moreover the default schema does not work with `angular schema form: ` []` should be removed for `items`:

```json
        "name": "owners",
          "owners": {
          "title": "Owners",
          "description": "Owners of the deposit (a list of user's IDs).",
          "type": "array",
          "name": "owners",
          "items": {
              "type": "integer"
            }
        },
```

Note: I'm note sure that `$ref` works with `angular schema form`  for `_files`.
 
At the end I still got errors:

- glyphicons
- `http://localhost:5000/deposit/undefined/toto.pdf ` 404 not found during the file uploads

As this module is quite complex and the doc is not yet completely written, it would be great to have a working example.

Thanks in advance.

```
Traceback (most recent call last):

    self.preferred = self.client.testConnection(self.conn)
```

issue_title: Send rate limiting can over-limit
issue_body:
In some cases, rate limiting can unnecessarily delay heaps, leading to dropped heaps. This particularly happens when heaps are small (e.g. 6KB), and are being queued at less than the rate limit.

I think what happens is that a number of heaps arrive, are queued and immediately sent. This ratchets up rate_bytes until it reaches the burst limit. Then there is a sleep put in to compensate for the burst, but send_time has *already* been advanced when the queue was found to be empty.

issue_title: Convert python functions into numexpr bytecode
issue_body: For now numexpr is not pythonic, we have to deal with strings, it's messy.  This can be fixed by transforming python functions into numexpr bytecode. Of course only a subset of all possible functions can be transformed. There are 2 ways: transform python bytecode or transform python AST. Since python bytecode is an impl detail which is to be changed, transforming AST is more compatible way, but it is limited to the cases where we can get  the original python source. But there are packages decompiling python source from bytecode. So since we have to deal with bytecode anyway, why not to deal witn it directly, using some libraries, dealing with differrences of bytecode format around versions?

issue_title: Alpha decomposition
issue_body:
As per @marketneutral's suggestion, it would be a fantastic addition to `alphalens` to implement the analysis done in @cfenaroli's [public research lecture](https://github.com/quantopian/research_public/blob/alt-data-case-studies/case_studies/USD_EUR_exchange_rate/notebook.ipynb) which was inspired by Exhibit 3 in [this AQR whitepaper](https://www.aqr.com/-/media/files/papers/measuring-factor-exposures-uses-and-abuses.pdf). Specifically, we should implement an "alpha decomposition" which looks like this:

![screen shot 2017-08-04 at 4 00 55 pm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19851673/28985007-8bd7ddba-792e-11e7-986c-40119127f20d.png)

To be more precise, given a factor risk model, we would like to see how much of the alpha's returns is explained by common risk factors, such as sector, momentum, etc. This would allow us to see how much of the alpha's returns is driven by genuine stock-specific reasons, and how much can be attributed to common factors.

issue_title: Django WSGIRequest's data is not sanitized at all by SanitizeProcessor
issue_body:
`python 3.6.2`
`Django==1.11.4`
`raven==6.1.0`

`DjangoClient` captures request data from `WSGIRequest`. It's `body` attribute holds a `bytes` object, so body is skipped in `SanitizeProcessor` cause it's not an instance of `string_types` which is `str` for `python3`. This might cause sensitive data to be sent to sentry server unmasked!

issue_title: Allow user to force bound on scores
issue_body:
Reported by @elisabettai.
Some features (e.g. inv_time_to_first_spike when first spike is very close to start of stimulus) can create very very high scores. This confuses the algorithm.
At the moment scores that don't generate an error are not bounded (when an error occurs the score is set to 250 though).
Option should be added to EFelFeature to allow the user to force a bound on the scores, and to allow to set the value of the bound.

issue_title: a-la guake-indicator custom commands
issue_body:
Feature: allow users to define there own commands they often use
This can work like guake-indicator, but not with the same level of customization. The source code is heavily using C code, and reimplement lot of trivial stuff in python world (json parsing, GTK event handling,...)

Inserting guake-indicator custom feature in guake has the following advantage:
- compatible with the "hide on focus loss" mode
- automatically available to the user

I also tend to prefere having a custom xml/json file, so the user can back it up easily, vs an import/export feature that is too heavy to use

issue_title: Do not crash when asked for a parameter after end date
issue_body:
As someone who use openfisca as a backend of a simulation application,
My application doesn't crash overnight without me having done anything,
Because we just passed the end date of one legislative parameter

----------------

The changes introduced with the fuzzy parameters are a catastrophe for anyone using openfisca in prod. It introduces a major production risk : if we pass the end date of a parameter we use, then the application cannot do any calculation anymore.

The PNDS (institutional re-user) application was down for some time because of this, as well as some of our testing instances.

Openfisca should offer a "production" mode where it is flexible and extrapolates parameters values, and a "strict" mode where it crashed when a value is missing if it is necessary.

issue_title: Remove dependency link on django-autoslug
issue_body:
As it is, `pip install --process-dependecy-links resolwe` fails when it is run the second time since pip uses the cached wheel it built the first time Resolwe was installed. Hence, it doesn't install Resolwe from the sdist release, which supports dependency links, but from binary/wheel release which doesn't support dependency links.

To resolve this, remove dependency link on django-autoslug and release a forked version on PyPI if the [issue](https://github.com/neithere/django-autoslug/pull/18) is not resolved by the next release.
